evals.name: cold-start-vegetarian-week
evals.prompt: I want to start meal prepping. Can you plan dinners for this week and make me a grocery list? It's just me and my partner, we're vegetarian, I really can't stand mushrooms, and on weeknights I've only got about 30 minutes to actually cook.
evals.expected_output: Sets up a profile (2 servings, vegetarian, no mushrooms, 30-min weeknights), creates the data dir + config, plans ~7 vegetarian dinners with no mushrooms anywhere and weeknight-appropriate times, saves each new recipe to recipes/ following the template, writes a dated plan to plans/, and produces a grocery list consolidated and grouped by store section.

evals.name: like-recipe-and-replan
evals.prompt: We made the Lemon-Garlic Chicken Thighs from my recipes last week and absolutely loved it — mark it as a favorite. Then plan 5 dinners for next week and work that one back in.
evals.expected_output: Finds the existing recipe, sets liked: true in its frontmatter and adds a star in index.md, then plans exactly 5 dinners that include the lemon-garlic chicken, reads the existing profile/DB, fills remaining slots from existing or new recipes, and outputs a grocery list. Pre-populated data dir provided as input.

evals.name: ingredient-reuse-family-of-four
evals.prompt: Plan 7 dinners for my family of 4. I want recipes that share ingredients so I'm not throwing out half a bunch of cilantro or a lonely can of coconut milk — minimize waste and overlap where you can. Give me the shopping list grouped by aisle.
evals.expected_output: Plans 7 dinners scaled to 4 servings, deliberately overlapping ingredients across recipes to reduce waste, and produces a grocery list that consolidates shared ingredients into single combined quantities, grouped by store section, with staples flagged separately. Saves recipes and plan to the data dir.
